title: Bridge Essay: How Interoception and the Insula Shape Mental Imagery and Aphantasia.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal** (Phase 10) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Imaginal to mechanistic neuroscience through How, Interoception, and, the, Insula.

**How Interoception and the Insula Shape Mental Imagery and Aphantasia.** — Brain Topogr. PMID: 39912831. DOI: 10.1007/s10548-025-01101-6. Amajor question in cognitive neuroscience is understanding the neural basis of mental imagery, particularly in cases of its absence, known as aphantasia. While research in this field has focused on the role of sensory domains, we propose that the key to understanding imagery lies in the intertwining of sensory processing and autonomic responses. Interoception plays acrucial role in mental imagery by anchoring experiences in first-person physiological signals, providing aself-referential persp
